reason: The item cleanly targets boundary-aware admissibility of single-step lumped modeling across a temperature regime, with correct choices constrained to regime-specific interpretations (multi-step earlier; single-dominant near Tsc; mechanism shift). Decoys are mostly single-fault and engineering-plausible, though two rely on broad, design-facing extrapolations that may feel stylistically more assertive and thus slightly easier to dismiss. Overall it offers strong expert discrimination without requiring extraneous calculations or outside assumptions.

The prompt tightly constrains both judgments to mechanism-linked boundaries (pressure source dominance at venting vs. separator collapse; proportionality change implying reaction-step structure), making the warranted conclusions hard to overextend without violating stated conditions. It also creates strong expert discrimination by forcing candidates to integrate pressure-component reasoning with model-form validity limits, while still leaving some room for mild linguistic latitude typical of short-answer grading.
